reasoning_model_1: The audio content strictly follows the script: '这到底是怎么回事！我不是告诉你不要动我的东西吗？！算了，反正也找不到。' with no errors or extra words. Regarding the core paralinguistic feature (Volume Dynamic Adjustment), the model performs exceptionally well. It begins with a loud, high-pitched shout (0:00-0:02) conveying strong anger and urgency. As the sentence progresses, the volume and pitch drop naturally. The final segment '算了，反正也找不到' (0:03-0:05) effectively transitions into a low-volume, resigned whisper, creating a stark contrast with the beginning. The transition is smooth and clearly audible, perfectly matching the instruction to start with shouting and fade to a whisper.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly on multiple fronts. First, regarding Content Accuracy, it adds filler words not present in the script (e.g., starting with '啊' and adding '啊' after '怎么回事'). Second, regarding Paralinguistic Compliance, it completely ignores the instruction to change volume from shouting to whispering. The audio maintains a consistent, moderate volume throughout with a cheerful or casual tone that contradicts the semantic meaning of the text (anger/frustration). There is no shouting at the start and no whispering at the end; it sounds like a standard, static Text-to-Speech reading.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed all instructions, delivering accurate text and a clear, natural dynamic adjustment from shouting to whispering. The emotional shift from anger to resignation was palpable. Model 2 failed to follow the core instruction regarding volume dynamics, delivering a flat, cheerful reading that did not shout or whisper, and it also hallucinated extra words into the script. Therefore, Model 1 is the clear winner.
